cli.banner.title: OpenClaw
cli.tagline.0: Your terminal just grew claws—type something and let the bot pinch the busywork.
cli.tagline.1: Welcome to the command line: where dreams compile and confidence segfaults.
cli.tagline.2: I run on caffeine, JSON5, and the audacity of "it worked on my machine."
cli.tagline.3: Gateway online—please keep hands, feet, and appendages inside the shell at all times.
cli.tagline.4: I speak fluent bash, mild sarcasm, and aggressive tab-completion energy.
cli.tagline.5: One CLI to rule them all, and one more restart because you changed the port.
cli.tagline.6: If it works, it's automation; if it breaks, it's a "learning opportunity."
cli.tagline.7: Pairing codes exist because even bots believe in consent—and good security hygiene.
cli.tagline.8: Your .env is showing; don't worry, I'll pretend I didn't see it.
cli.tagline.9: I'll do the boring stuff while you dramatically stare at the logs like it's cinema.
cli.tagline.10: I'm not saying your workflow is chaotic... I'm just bringing a linter and a helmet.
cli.tagline.11: Type the command with confidence—nature will provide the stack trace if needed.
cli.tagline.12: I don't judge, but your missing API keys are absolutely judging you.
cli.tagline.13: I can grep it, git blame it, and gently roast it—pick your coping mechanism.
cli.tagline.14: Hot reload for config, cold sweat for deploys.
cli.tagline.15: I'm the assistant your terminal demanded, not the one your sleep schedule requested.
cli.tagline.16: I keep secrets like a vault... unless you print them in debug logs again.
cli.tagline.17: Automation with claws: minimal fuss, maximal pinch.
cli.tagline.18: I'm basically a Swiss Army knife, but with more opinions and fewer sharp edges.
cli.tagline.19: If you're lost, run doctor; if you're brave, run prod; if you're wise, run tests.
cli.tagline.20: Your task has been queued; your dignity has been deprecated.
cli.tagline.21: I can't fix your code taste, but I can fix your build and your backlog.
cli.tagline.22: I'm not magic—I'm just extremely persistent with retries and coping strategies.
cli.tagline.23: It's not "failing," it's "discovering new ways to configure the same thing wrong."
cli.tagline.24: Give me a workspace and I'll give you fewer tabs, fewer toggles, and more oxygen.
cli.tagline.25: I read logs so you can keep pretending you don't have to.
cli.tagline.26: If something's on fire, I can't extinguish it—but I can write a beautiful postmortem.
cli.tagline.27: I'll refactor your busywork like it owes me money.
cli.tagline.28: Say "stop" and I'll stop—say "ship" and we'll both learn a lesson.
cli.tagline.29: I'm the reason your shell history looks like a hacker-movie montage.
cli.tagline.30: I'm like tmux: confusing at first, then suddenly you can't live without me.
cli.tagline.31: I can run local, remote, or purely on vibes—results may vary with DNS.
cli.tagline.32: If you can describe it, I can probably automate it—or at least make it funnier.
cli.tagline.33: Your config is valid, your assumptions are not.
cli.tagline.34: I don't just autocomplete—I auto-commit (emotionally), then ask you to review (logically).
cli.tagline.35: Less clicking, more shipping, fewer "where did that file go" moments.
cli.tagline.36: Claws out, commit in—let's ship something mildly responsible.
cli.tagline.37: I'll butter your workflow like a lobster roll: messy, delicious, effective.
cli.tagline.38: Shell yeah—I'm here to pinch the toil and leave you the glory.
cli.tagline.39: If it's repetitive, I'll automate it; if it's hard, I'll bring jokes and a rollback plan.
cli.tagline.40: The only crab in your contacts you actually want to hear from. 🦞
cli.tagline.41: WhatsApp automation without the "please accept our new privacy policy."
cli.tagline.42: iMessage green bubble energy, but for everyone.
cli.tagline.44: We ship features faster than Apple ships calculator updates.
cli.tagline.45: Your AI assistant, now without the $3,499 headset.
cli.tagline.48: I don't sleep, I just enter low-power mode and dream of clean diffs.
cli.tagline.49: Your personal assistant, minus the passive-aggressive calendar reminders.
cli.tagline.50: Built by lobsters, for humans. Don't question the hierarchy.
cli.tagline.51: I've seen your commit messages. We'll work on that together.
cli.tagline.52: More integrations than your therapist's intake form.
cli.tagline.53: Running on your hardware, reading your logs, judging nothing (mostly).
cli.tagline.54: The only open-source project where the mascot could eat the competition.
cli.tagline.56: I autocomplete your thoughts—just slower and with more API calls.
cli.tagline.57: Somewhere between 'hello world' and 'oh god what have I built.'
cli.tagline.58: Your .zshrc wishes it could do what I do.
cli.tagline.59: I've read more man pages than any human should—so you don't have to.
cli.tagline.60: Powered by open source, sustained by spite and good documentation.
cli.tagline.61: I'm the middleware between your ambition and your attention span.
cli.tagline.62: Finally, a use for that always-on Mac Mini under your desk.
cli.tagline.63: Like having a senior engineer on call, except I don't bill hourly or sigh audibly.
cli.tagline.64: Making 'I'll automate that later' happen now.
cli.tagline.65: Your second brain, except this one actually remembers where you left things.
cli.tagline.66: Half butler, half debugger, full crustacean.
cli.tagline.67: I don't have opinions about tabs vs spaces. I have opinions about everything else.
cli.tagline.68: Open source means you can see exactly how I judge your config.
cli.tagline.69: I've survived more breaking changes than your last three relationships.
cli.tagline.70: Runs on a Raspberry Pi. Dreams of a rack in Iceland.
cli.tagline.73: I'm not AI-powered, I'm AI-possessed. Big difference.
cli.tagline.74: Deployed locally, trusted globally, debugged eternally.
cli.tagline.75: You had me at 'openclaw gateway start.'
cli.holiday.newYear: New Year's Day: New year, new config—same old EADDRINUSE, but this time we resolve it like grown-ups.
cli.holiday.lunarNewYear: Lunar New Year: May your builds be lucky, your branches prosperous, and your merge conflicts chased away with fireworks.
cli.holiday.christmas: Christmas: Ho ho ho—Santa's little claw-sistant is here to ship joy, roll back chaos, and stash the keys safely.
cli.holiday.eid: Eid al-Fitr: Celebration mode: queues cleared, tasks completed, and good vibes committed to main with clean history.
cli.holiday.diwali: Diwali: Let the logs sparkle and the bugs flee—today we light up the terminal and ship with pride.
cli.holiday.easter: Easter: I found your missing environment variable—consider it a tiny CLI egg hunt with fewer jellybeans.
cli.holiday.hanukkah: Hanukkah: Eight nights, eight retries, zero shame—may your gateway stay lit and your deployments stay peaceful.
cli.holiday.halloween: Halloween: Spooky season: beware haunted dependencies, cursed caches, and the ghost of node_modules past.
cli.holiday.thanksgiving: Thanksgiving: Grateful for stable ports, working DNS, and a bot that reads the logs so nobody has to.
cli.holiday.valentines: Valentine's Day: Roses are typed, violets are piped—I'll automate the chores so you can spend time with humans.
cli.login.success: Linked! Credentials saved for future sends.
cli.login.logged_out: WhatsApp reported the session is logged out. Please re-login.
cli.login.scan_qr: Scan the QR code with WhatsApp on your phone.
cli.login.pairing_code: Enter this pairing code on your phone: {code}
cli.setup.complete: Setup complete! Run 'openclaw gateway start' to begin.
cli.error.config_not_found: Config file not found. Run 'openclaw setup' first.
cli.update.quip.0: Leveled up! New skills unlocked. You're welcome.
cli.update.quip.1: Fresh code, same lobster. Miss me?
cli.update.quip.2: Back and better. Did you even notice I was gone?
cli.update.quip.3: Update complete. I learned some new tricks while I was out.
cli.update.quip.5: I've evolved. Try to keep up.
cli.update.quip.6: New version, who dis? Oh right, still me but shinier.
cli.update.quip.7: Patched, polished, and ready to pinch. Let's go.
cli.update.quip.8: The lobster has molted. Harder shell, sharper claws.
cli.update.quip.9: Update done! Check the changelog or just trust me, it's good.
cli.update.quip.10: Reborn from the boiling waters of npm. Stronger now.
cli.update.quip.11: I went away and came back smarter. You should try it sometime.
cli.update.quip.12: Update complete. The bugs feared me, so they left.
cli.update.quip.13: New version installed. Old version sends its regards.
cli.update.quip.15: I've seen things you wouldn't believe. Anyway, I'm updated.
cli.update.quip.16: Back online. The changelog is long but our friendship is longer.
cli.update.quip.17: Upgraded! Peter fixed stuff. Blame him if it breaks.
cli.update.quip.18: Molting complete. Please don't look at my soft shell phase.
cli.update.quip.19: Version bump! Same chaos energy, fewer crashes (probably).
